news_items.text:
Team News: Iraq vs. Norway injury, suspension list, predicted XIs | World Cup 2026 - Sports Mole

Menu

World Cup Gameweek 1

Boston Stadium

# Team News: Iraq vs. Norway injury, suspension list, predicted XIs | World Cup 2026

By Lewis Nolan| 15 Jun 2026 23:00, Last updated: 15 Jun 2026 23:00

© Iconsport / Brooks Von Arx

Iraq and Norway will begin their 2026 World Cup campaigns at Boston Stadium on Tuesday, when the two meet in their opening game of Group I.

Erling Haaland will be keen to add to his impressive goal tally for Norway, but he will have to get the better of opposition goalkeeper Jalal Hassan.

Here, Sports Mole rounds up the team news for both nations.

---

## IRAQ vs. NORWAY

## IRAQ

Out: None

Doubtful: None

Sports Mole's predicted XI: Hassan; Ali, Tahseen, Sulaka, Doski; Al-Ammari, Iqbal; Bayesh, Jasim, Al-Hamadi; Hussein

## NORWAY

Out: None

Doubtful: None

Sports Mole's predicted XI: Nyland; Ryerson, Ajer, Heggem, Wolfe; Odegaard, Berge, Aursnes; Sorloth, Haaland, Nusa

---

Written by

Share this article:

Subscribe to our newsletter

news_items.title: Iraq vs Norway FIFA World Cup 2026 Preview: Everything you need to know | Goal.com US
news_items.author: Celine Abrahams
news_items.text:
Iraq vs Norway FIFA World Cup 2026 Preview: Everything you need to know | Goal.com US

WATCH SECURELY FROM ANYWHERE

Jun 16, 2026 10:55+03:00

TV Guide & Streaming

GOAL brings you everything you need to know about Iraq vs Norway at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

World Cup - Grp. I

Boston Stadium

Iraq vs Norway will kick-off on 16 June 2026 at 22:00 GMT and 18:00 EST.

Getty Images

## Iraq vs Norway: Match context

The match in Massachusetts carries huge significance for two teams determined to make a strong start in one of the tournament’s most competitive groups. With expectations mounting at home, Iraq coach Graham Arnold will be looking to demonstrate that his absurdly hard-working, structured squad can surprise the world on football’s biggest stage, relying on a disciplined collective identity and the talismanic threat of forward Aymen Hussein. Standing in their way is a highly explosive and physically dominant Norway side led by Ståle Solbakken, whose tactical system centres around unlocking the world-class firepower of Erling Haaland and Martin Ødegaard to systematically dismantle opponents. Set against the backdrop of the impressive Boston Stadium (Gillette Stadium), with its

news_items.title: How to Watch Iraq vs Norway Live Today: World Cup Preview, Team News, Stats, Prediction
news_items.author: Dogli Wilberforce
news_items.text:
# How to Watch Iraq vs Norway Live Today: World Cup Preview, Team News, Stats, Prediction

Author: Dogli Wilberforce
Published: 2026-06-16T16:28:56+00:00
Source: heavy.com (heavy.com)
Language: en

## Story

How to Watch Iraq vs Norway Live Today: World Cup Preview, Team News, Stats, Prediction

- 0 Shares

By

- Updated Jun 16, 2026 at 12:28pm

Getty

(COMBO) This combination of file photos created on Iraq's midfielder Amjad Attwan in Al Wakrah, on September 7, 2021 (L); and Norway's captain #09 Erling Braut Haaland in Milan, on November 16, 2025. Iraq and Norway will meet in a 2026 World Cup Group I football match at the Boston Stadium in Foxborough on June 16, 2026. (Photo by Stefano RELLANDINI and Karim JAAFAR / AFP via Getty Images)

Iraq vs Norway, live today, opens Group I at the 2026 FIFA World Cup, with both nations carrying the weight of history into Boston. Iraq returns to football’s biggest stage for the first time in 40 years and is making only its second World Cup appearance. Norway also ends a long wait, returning to the tournament after a 28-year absence.

With France and Senegal also in Group I, every point is likely to be crucial in the race for the knockout round

# Team News: Iraq vs. Norway injury, suspension list, predicted XIs | World Cup 2026

By Lewis Nolan| 15 Jun 2026 23:00, Last updated: 15 Jun 2026 23:00

© Iconsport / Brooks Von Arx

Iraq and Norway will begin their 2026 World Cup campaigns at Boston Stadium on Tuesday, when the two meet in their opening game of Group I.

Erling Haaland will be keen to add to his impressive goal tally for Norway, but he will have to get the better of opposition goalkeeper Jalal Hassan.

Here, Sports Mole rounds up the team news for both nations.

---

## IRAQ vs. NORWAY

## IRAQ

Out: None

Doubtful: None

Sports Mole's predicted XI: Hassan; Ali, Tahseen, Sulaka, Doski; Al-Ammari, Iqbal; Bayesh, Jasim, Al-Hamadi; Hussein

## NORWAY

Out: None

Doubtful: None

Sports Mole's predicted XI: Nyland; Ryerson, Ajer, Heggem, Wolfe; Odegaard, Berge, Aursnes; Sorloth, Haaland, Nusa

---

Written by

Share this article:

GOAL brings you everything you need to know about Iraq vs Norway at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

World Cup - Grp. I

Boston Stadium

Iraq vs Norway will kick-off on 16 June 2026 at 22:00 GMT and 18:00 EST.

Getty Images

## Iraq vs Norway: Match context

The match in Massachusetts carries huge significance for two teams determined to make a strong start in one of the tournament’s most competitive groups. With expectations mounting at home, Iraq coach Graham Arnold will be looking to demonstrate that his absurdly hard-working, structured squad can surprise the world on football’s biggest stage, relying on a disciplined collective identity and the talismanic threat of forward Aymen Hussein. Standing in their way is a highly explosive and physically dominant Norway side led by Ståle Solbakken, whose tactical system centres around unlocking the world-class firepower of Erling Haaland and Martin Ødegaard to systematically dismantle opponents. Set against the backdrop of the impressive Boston Stadium (Gillette Stadium), with its"}

## Story

Getty

(COMBO) This combination of file photos created on Iraq's midfielder Amjad Attwan in Al Wakrah, on September 7, 2021 (L); and Norway's captain #09 Erling Braut Haaland in Milan, on November 16, 2025. Iraq and Norway will meet in a 2026 World Cup Group I football match at the Boston Stadium in Foxborough on June 16, 2026. (Photo by Stefano RELLANDINI and Karim JAAFAR / AFP via Getty Images)

Iraq vs Norway, live today, opens Group I at the 2026 FIFA World Cup, with both nations carrying the weight of history into Boston. Iraq returns to football’s biggest stage for the first time in 40 years and is making only its second World Cup appearance. Norway also ends a long wait, returning to the tournament after a 28-year absence.

With France and Senegal also in Group I, every point is likely to be crucial in the race for the knockout round"}
